question: Find the range of this data set: 3, 7, 12, 15, 22.
Find the range of this data set: 45, 50, 55, 60, 65, 70.
Data set: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14. Find the IQR (interquartile range).
hints: Find Q1 (median of lower half) and Q3 (median of upper half). IQR = Q3 − Q1.
solution: Median = 8. Lower half: 2,4,6 → Q1 = 4. Upper half: 10,12,14 → Q3 = 12. IQR = 12 − 4 = 8.

question: Data set: 5, 8, 10, 12, 15. The mean is 10. Find the MAD (mean absolute deviation).
hints: Find each distance from the mean: |5−10|, |8−10|, |10−10|, |12−10|, |15−10|.
Then find the mean of those distances.

question: Data set A: 10, 10, 10, 10, 10. Data set B: 2, 6, 10, 14, 18. Both have mean 10. Which has greater variability? Find the MAD of each to support your answer.
answer: B, MAD of A is 0 and MAD of B is 4.8
hints: For A, every value equals the mean, so all deviations are 0.
solution: A: all deviations = 0, MAD = 0. B: deviations are 8,4,0,4,8. Sum = 24. MAD = 24/5 = 4.8. B has greater variability.
